title: Hodge theory for non-Archimedean analytic spaces
speaker: Vladimir Berkovich
abstract: By Deligne's Hodge theory, the integral cohomology groups H^n(X^h, Z) of the C-analytification of a separated scheme X of finite type over C are provided with a mixed Hodge structure, functorial in X. Given a non-Archimedean field K isomorphic to the field of Laurent power series C((z)), there is a functor X \mapsto X^an_K that takes X to the non-Archimedean K-analytification of X_K = X \otimes_C K. I'll describe a Hodge theory for the full subcategory of the category of K-analytic spaces that consists of the strictly K-analytic spaces with the property that each compact analytic subdomain is isomorphic to an analytic domain in a boundaryless space. It extends Deligne's Hodge theory through the above functor and generalizes previously known complex analytic constructions.

proof_stages.label: Deligne mixed-Hodge template
proof_stages.summary: The talk recalls Deligne's mixed Hodge structures on cohomology of complex algebraic varieties, with weight filtration, Hodge filtration via de Rham cohomology, and the comparison isomorphism as the classical pattern to be generalized.

proof_stages.label: Non-Archimedean setting and target arena
proof_stages.summary: The speaker fixes a complete discretely valued non-Archimedean field k with residue field mathbb C, passes from complex varieties to k-analytic spaces, and introduces the punctured residue-tangent space together with linearly compact local systems as the eventual target.

proof_stages.label: Betti local systems from nearby cycles
proof_stages.summary: For every degree n a functorial rational cohomology local system H^n_Q is constructed on strict k-analytic spaces using special formal models and nearby-cycle complexes, with finiteness for confined spaces, constancy on complex algebraic varieties, and projective-limit behavior over compact domains.

proof_stages.label: De Rham cohomology as distinguished D-modules
proof_stages.summary: The de Rham side is formulated as a functor H^n_dR to linearly compact distinguished D-modules over k, after defining connections, D-lattices, residues and spectra, and relating the construction to usual de Rham cohomology in smooth confined cases.

proof_stages.label: Non-Archimedean comparison isomorphism
proof_stages.summary: A distinguished D-module is converted into a local system on the punctured residue-tangent space by using the canonical residue lattice and horizontal sections, and the theorem gives a canonical isomorphism between this local system and H^n_Q.

proof_stages.label: Mixed Hodge structures over k
proof_stages.summary: A finite distinguished mixed Hodge structure over k is defined as rational local-system data with a weight filtration, a distinguished D-module with a Hodge filtration on its residue space, a monodromy condition, and a compatibility isomorphism producing a classical variation of mixed Hodge structures.

proof_stages.label: Functorial Hodge theory on embeddable analytic spaces
proof_stages.summary: For k-analytic spaces whose compact domains embed in boundaryless spaces, the constructed cohomology data are assembled into a functorial distinguished mixed Hodge structure, with properties such as weight-zero topological cohomology and recovery of Deligne's structures for complex algebraic varieties.

proof_stages.label: Known limit cases and proof inputs
proof_stages.summary: The construction is compared with existing mixed Hodge structures on nearby-cycle cohomology, and the speaker explains that formal-scheme resolution of singularities and nearby-cycle comparisons underlie the treatment of singularities.

examples.name: Laurent-series model for the ground field
examples.note: Choosing a uniformizer identifies the valued field noncanonically with a Laurent series field over mathbb C, making the abstract hypotheses on k concrete but not driving the construction.
examples.engagement_reason: he spells out the Laurent-series model via a uniformizer, describing the valuation ring, maximal ideal, residue field, the induced isomorphism with C((z)), and later the differential/connection formula in that coordinate

examples.name: Boundary test cases
examples.note: Algebraic analytifications have empty boundary, while a closed disk in the affine line has the Gauss point as boundary, illustrating the boundary condition in the theorem.

examples.name: Saito-type nearby-cycle degeneration
examples.note: A complex analytic family over a disk with a closed subspace gives mixed Hodge structures on nearby- or vanishing-cycle cohomology, presented as a known limiting case recovered by the non-Archimedean construction.
examples.engagement_reason: he traces the Saito-type degeneration through the nearby/vanishing-cycle setup, constructs the associated completed formal scheme and generic fiber, and identifies the resulting group as a stalk
